assertions.evidence: Quantize command requires model in 'original' subdirectory but download command stores files directly in model directory. CLI shows error: 'Model sshleifer/tiny-gpt2 not found in cache' even after successful download.
assertions.error: Mismatch between download storage format and quantize expected format. quantize_model() expects files in <model_dir>/original/ but download_model() stores directly in <model_dir>/

assertions.evidence: Cannot test 8-bit quantization because 4-bit quantization fails with the same model discovery issue.
assertions.error: Blocked by VAL-CLI-004 failure - same root cause prevents testing

assertions.evidence: Cannot test KV cache quantization because base quantization fails.

Tested with 'llm-compress quantize nonexistent/model --bits 4'. Command correctly exits with code 1 and shows error: 'Model nonexistent/model not found in cache. Please download it first with: llm-compress download nonexistent/model'

frictions: Download and quantize commands use different directory structures for model storage
No integration test exists for the full download -> quantize workflow
blockers: Model directory structure mismatch prevents CLI quantize from working
